claim_text: LLM-assisted literature review reduces time to synthesize 100 papers by approximately 60% compared to manual review
notes: RCT with 24 graduate students; manual vs GPT-4-assisted condition; statistically significant (p<0.01)

claim_text: Automated citation extraction from PDFs using LLMs achieves >90% F1 on structured scientific documents
notes: Evaluated on PubMed and arXiv corpora; human annotator agreement reported (kappa=0.87)

claim_text: LLM-generated summaries of scientific abstracts contain factual errors at a rate of 12–18% when evaluated by domain experts
notes: Expert panel of 15 reviewers; hallucination rate rises to 28% for methods sections; key limitation of C001

claim_text: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) reduces hallucination rate in scientific summarization by approximately 40% relative to standard prompting
notes: Tested on 3 benchmarks; improvement varies by domain (22–55%); no statistical significance tests reported

claim_text: Human researchers rate LLM-assisted literature syntheses as equally credible to human-written syntheses in double-blind evaluation
notes: n=40 evaluators; only abstracts evaluated not full sections; potential demand characteristics

claim_text: GPT-4 correctly identifies the methodology of a paper (qualitative vs quantitative) with 94% accuracy on a held-out test set
notes: Binary classification task; may not generalize to mixed-methods papers

claim_text: LLM assistance in literature review does not significantly reduce missed relevant papers compared to manual keyword search
notes: Directly challenges C001; recall metric differs from C001 time-efficiency metric; both claims can coexist

claim_text: Chain-of-thought prompting improves LLM accuracy on evidence quality assessment tasks from 61% to 79%
notes: Improvement measured on custom rubric; rubric not independently validated; potential annotation bias

claim_text: LLMs trained on scientific corpora perform no better than general LLMs on domain-specific claim verification tasks
notes: Only two domain-adapted models tested; small evaluation set (n=200 claims); contradicts common assumption

claim_text: A structured evidence matrix protocol combined with LLM-assisted screening reduces duplicate claim registration by 73% versus unstructured note-taking
notes: Single-group study at one institution; no control condition; methodology relevant to this OS design
